records.ety: [F. caparaçon, fr. Sp. caparazon a cover for a saddle, coach, etc.; capa cloak, cover (fr. LL. capa, cf. LL. caparo also fr. capa) + the term. azon. See Cap.]

defs: An ornamental covering or housing for a horse; the harness or trappings of a horse, taken collectively, especially when decorative.
quotes: Their horses clothed with rich caparison.
My heart groans beneath the gay caparison.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Caparisoned (?) p. pr. & vb. n. Caparisoning.]

defs: To cover with housings, as a horse; to harness or fit out with decorative trappings, as a horse.
quotes: The steeds, caparisoned with purple, stand.

defs: To adorn with rich dress; to dress.
quotes: I am caparisoned like a man.
